result:
I’ve been thinking about the nature of small moments lately—the kinds of quiet, unremarkable bits of time that rarely make it into photos or stories, yet shape how we move through the world. There’s something special about the first sip of coffee in the morning, or the pause before stepping out the door when the weather is a mystery. Those little rituals, even if they don’t feel like much, help us anchor ourselves when everything else feels unpredictable.

In the same way, I like to imagine the world as an ongoing conversation we’re all contributing to. Every small note—what you read, what you notice, the way you respond to someone—builds into something larger. Maybe that’s why many of us write, or talk, or take pictures: to gather these fragments and offer them back, in our own voice.

We still have lots of time to dig deeper into whatever’s on your mind, whether it’s something grand or tiny.
